cycle_context: Cycle 9 — 3 codex security fixes: changelog-analyst.md ABORT + orphan-skip logic; pretool-bash-safety.sh ANSI-C normalize; tests stale-token replacements

out_of_scope: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — bash hook executable, not a prompt document
scope_decision.scope_rationale: Prompt-inspector pattern catalog (Philosophy, Overview, Quality Standards, Helper Scripts, verbose Usage/Examples) applies to markdown command/agent LLM-instruction documents. Bash hooks, python tests, and JSON manifests are executable artifacts evaluated by qa-inspector / test runners, not prompt docs.

hunks.change: Replaced 4-line WARNING fallback with 5-line ABORT block enforcing scope_violation when no dev-report exists
hunks.notes: Inserted text: ABORT message + structured failure JSON + 1-line enforcement rationale. All content is execution-required for the new fail-loud behavior.

hunks.change: Replaced 9-line orphan auto-commit template with 14-line skip-orphan procedural rule (BULK=false vs BULK=true conditional)
hunks.notes: Inserted text: 2 mode-conditioned WARNING messages + 1-line rationale tying skip behavior to cross-session contamination prevention. All content drives the new skip-not-commit behavior.

diff_analysis.verdict: No verbose meta-sections (Philosophy/Overview/Quality Standards/Helper Scripts/Examples) present in file. Diff additions are short rationale sentences attached to enforcement rules — qualify as procedural justification (the 'why' a rule fires), not 'stories' (narrative explanation divorced from execution). Diff introduces ZERO new verbose violations; introduced_in_diff:false marks this file-level finding as carrying no NEW verbosity per the Mechanism-(ii) tagging contract.

findings.recommendation: No action required. Diff content is fully procedural and consistent with the file's existing rules-not-stories style.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed empty-findings verdict is correct. All 5 codex items were OBSERVATION_ONLY (items 1-4 with no PROPOSED_FIX; item 5 carried a minor wording PROPOSED_FIX): (1) procedural ABORT/skip rules are not verbose non-functional content; (2) two rationale sentences are concise rule justifications tied to enforcement, not stories; (3) no severity to assign with findings carrying introduced_in_diff:false; (4) hook/.sh, tests, manifests correctly excluded from prompt-inspector scope; (5) wording nit — file-level summary should use 'new_verbose_violations:0' since introduced_in_diff is a finding-level marker, not a file-level summary field.

findings.issue: Draft empty findings verdict for THIS diff
findings.rationale: Codex confirms verdict; no change required.

findings.issue: Two 1-line rationale sentences in inserted hunks — could they be 'stories'?
findings.rationale: Codex confirms these are concise rule justifications tied directly to enforcement behavior, not narrative stories. They qualify as procedural justification under rules-not-stories.
With finding carrying introduced_in_diff:false there is no NEW-in-this-diff severity to assign; the changelog-analyst finding object carries severity:'none' for explicitness.

findings.issue: Should hook .sh / tests / manifests be inspected as prompt docs?
findings.rationale: Codex confirms scope exclusion: prompt-inspector evaluates markdown command/agent LLM-instruction docs only.

findings.issue: Wording: file-level summary phrasing of introduced_in_diff verdict
findings.proposed_fix: Use 'new_verbose_violations:0' at the summary level; reserve introduced_in_diff as a finding-level marker per the Mechanism-(ii) tagging contract.
findings.rationale: Applied: summary uses 'new_verbose_violations:0'. The changelog-analyst.md finding retains introduced_in_diff:false per the inspector's mandatory Mechanism-(ii) tagging contract for --changed-files mode.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Minor wording PROPOSED_FIX from item 5 applied (summary uses new_verbose_violations:0). All other items were OBSERVATION_ONLY with no fix needed — codex affirmed the empty-findings verdict, scope exclusions, and rationale-vs-story classification.
